# Why Staged Autonomy Beats Full Autonomy

Source file: `raw/staged-autonomy-thread.md`

## Summary

Karpathy argues that LLM-maintained wikis need staged autonomy: human
oversight early, earned LLM autonomy later. The `explored: false` flag
is the visible marker of the trust curve.

## Key Claims

- Bookkeeping, not reading or thinking, is the tedious part of wiki maintenance
- LLMs don't get bored — they can handle 15 file updates per pass
- One-shot autonomy on a new domain produces messy ontology
- Human-in-loop for the first ~N sources establishes the schema
- The `explored: false` frontmatter flag tracks which pages a human has reviewed
